file_level.general_notes: Best practices score: 10/10 (0 FAIL, 0 WARN, 6 PASS)

rules.detail: File is well-formed JSON. No trailing commas, no comments, all keys and string values are properly double-quoted.
No API keys, passwords, tokens, or any sensitive credentials are present. The package contains only standard npm metadata fields.
Excluded per project instructions — package.json always receives PASS for naming convention, as all keys follow the npm-mandated camelCase standard (devDependencies, private, etc.).
Maximum nesting depth is 2 levels (top-level fields → scripts and devDependencies values), well within the 5-level readability limit.
All keys are fully descriptive standard npm fields: name, version, private, type, description, main, scripts, license, devDependencies. No single-letter keys or cryptic abbreviations.
File uses consistent 2-space indentation throughout. Not minified. Each key-value pair is on its own line with no irregularities.
